Class OutboxConfigurationException

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Throwable
java.lang.Exception
java.lang.RuntimeException
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.OutboxException
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.OutboxConfigurationException
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class OutboxConfigurationException extends OutboxException
Signals that the outbox cannot operate because the deployment is misconfigured: the outbox table is missing, columns do not match the expected schema, the JDBC driver is not on the classpath, or the configured DataSource cannot be opened at all.

What the caller should do: fail fast, ideally at application startup. Do not retry — the condition will not resolve itself without an operator action (apply the migration, fix the connection string, deploy the right driver). Surface the exception so the deployment is rejected.

Dialects classify SQLState classes 42 (syntax error or access rule violation, e.g. undefined table 42P01 on PostgreSQL) and 3D (invalid catalog name) as configuration failures.

Since:
0.1.0
See Also:

    Description
